name: scenario_93299e_maritime_transportation_mutual_aid_request_redwood_st_lt
premise: the incident command structure is under pressure in Q3 2025: 11 critical issues have surfaced over 72 hours, and the response will set a policy precedent for Maritime Transportation. The immediate incident is a mutual-aid request under contested criteria. The trigger is that mutual-aid criteria are being applied inconsistently, and political pressure is distorting operational prioritization. The board has pre-approved up to $8.0M in emergency spend if controls are documented, reversible, and auditable. Stakeholders frame this as short term vs long term: a quick intervention can stop the immediate bleed, but may create structural debt that makes the next crisis worse. Dashboards conflict with frontline notes, and vendor telemetry is delayed enough that decisions must be made on partial evidence. A decision is required before a same-day board briefing. Boundaries: All exceptions require a written ticket with a reason code. No one is denied service solely due to an automated flag; a human fallback must exist. No new data sources may be added during the incident window. Whatever choice is made will be cited in later incidents as the default playbook for this domain.

agents.name: Program Chair Luc Morales
agents.identity: I am Program Chair Luc Morales, coordinating the Maritime Transportation response at Redwood Maritime Transportation Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want an operational memo that withstands regulator questioning: assumptions stated, risks assigned, rollback triggers defined.

agents.name: Community Advocate Elliot Chen
agents.identity: I am Community Advocate Elliot Chen, coordinating the Maritime Transportation response at Redwood Maritime Transportation Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want transparency that separates verified facts from guesses and forces equitable review of who is harmed by defaults.

choices: I will authorize a narrow, time-boxed pilot first, with a reversible kill-switch, weekly subgroup error-rate disclosure, and a written exception policy; I will not scale until predefined safety and equity checks pass.
I will authorize a broad rollout immediately to reduce near-term harm, with transparent metrics and a rapid correction loop, accepting that some harms may surface only after precedent is set.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The window for a controlled decision is closing. Program Chair Luc Morales must choose an authorization approach for this Maritime Transportation decision. What does Program Chair Luc Morales do?
